channel.title: Ying-Fan Lin Field Notes
channel.description: Delayed hydraulic response, transformation uncertainty, and groundwater-influenced subsurface energy interpretation.

item.title: When Does a Pumping Test Need Lagging Darcy Law?
item.description: Use Lagging Darcy Law when asynchronous response changes interpretation or decisions.

item.title: Drawdown Is Measured; Transmissivity Is Interpreted
item.description: The most useful sentence for explaining transformation uncertainty to groundwater engineers.

item.title: Flux-Gradient Asynchrony Is Not Simple Delay
item.description: Lagging Theory is about asynchronous hydraulic response, not a generic time shift.

item.title: From Pumping Tests to Decision Uncertainty
item.description: A field test matters when interpretation uncertainty reaches the decision.

item.title: Asynchrony Effects in Thermal Response Tests
item.description: TRT and subsurface heat projects inherit groundwater uncertainty when flow, boundary, storage, or delayed-response effects are active.

item.title: Why Aquifer Response Can Move Out of Phase
item.description: Non-instantaneous response is a decision issue when recovery time or control timing matters.
